subject: Integrated Science
style: Uganda flag-inspired classroom infographic; exact SVG text overlay; no generated text inside raster image.
title: The Human Body: Muscular-Skeletal System
labels: Why Do We Have a Shape?
details: The body has a firm framework.
The skeleton is the bony framework of the body.
The system is called the Muscular-Skeletal System.
evidence_excerpts: Prior knowledge activation: Discussing why the body has a firm shape and how it moves. The learner explains the purpose of the internal body framework. The body has a firm framework.; This framework allows us to stand and move.; Without it, we would be like a heap of cloth.

Exploration: Observing a skeleton chart to see how bones are arranged. The learner identifies the major parts of the human skeleton on a chart. The skeleton is the bony framework of the body.; It is made of many bones of different shapes.; It supports the whole body.

Concept Introduction: Defining Bones, Muscles, and Joints. The learner identifies the three main components of the muscular-skeletal system. Bones: The hard framework.; Muscles: The soft tissues that pull bones.; Joints: The places where two or more bones meet.
